response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with deep expertise in TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web/distributed systems, but the role specifically demands a hands-on C++ developer with STL, multi-threaded systems, message brokers, IoT protocols (MQTT/Modbus/OPC UA), and IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity experience. C++ exposure is limited to early-career work at National Instruments (2000-2001) and a bachelor thesis, with no recent industrial automation, secure SDL, or IEC 62443 background. Skills section omits C++ entirely, and the resume shows no message-broker or industrial-protocol work.
strengths: 20+ years of software engineering with full-stack and distributed-systems exposure
Strong testing/TDD culture and mentoring/workshop experience aligned with team-mentoring duties
Some low-latency, high-throughput backend work (Sociomantic micro-services, async I/O)
concerns: No recent or substantial C++/STL production experience; C++ absent from skills list
No industrial automation, IEC 62443 SDL, or IoT-protocol (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA) background
Resume contains inconsistent/overlapping employment dates undermining credibility

key_factors.factor: Breadth of engineering experience and mentoring
